div.head: The Ambassador in Chile (Collier) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 38. Application for customs free entry aeroplane and accessories Captain O’Neill denied but will be admitted under bond canceled upon leaving Chile. Expect the same response similar application for Lieutenant Wade,Lt. Leigh Wade, representative of the Consolidated Aircraft Corporation; see pp. 811 ff. grounds commercial undertaking. Have not requested customs free entry for Curtiss aeroplanes and accessories as Department did not so instruct but believe Chilean Government would also treat as commercial venture. Have advised Wade, Lima; Doolittle,Lt. James Doolittle, with Department of Commerce experimental and path-finding flight to South America; see pp. 811 ff. La Paz; and SomersPresumably James D. Summers, aeronautical trade commissioner of the Department of Commerce for Latin America. and O’Neill, Rio de Janeiro, accordingly.
